    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 93
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/93](https://www.erdosproblems.com/93)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’If $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are in convex position (vertices of a convex polygon), then they
    25вҶ’determine at least $\lfloor n/2 \rfloor$ distinct distances. Proved by Altman.
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’[Al63] Altman, E., _On a problem of P. ErdЕ‘s_. Amer. Math. Monthly 70 (1963), 148-157.
    28вҶ’-/
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’open scoped Classical
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’namespace Erdos93
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’/--
    35вҶ’A finite set of points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ is in convex position if no point lies in the
    36вҶ’convex hull of the remaining points. Equivalently, the points are the vertices
    37вҶ’of a convex polygon.
    38вҶ’-/
    39вҶ’def InConvexPosition (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    40вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ A, p вҲү convexHull в„қ ((вҶ‘A : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) \ {p})
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’/--
    43вҶ’The set of distinct pairwise distances between distinct points in a finite
    44вҶ’point set in $\mathbb{R}^2$.
    45вҶ’-/
    46вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Finset в„қ :=
    47вҶ’  A.offDiag.image (fun pq => dist pq.1 pq.2)
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’/--
    50вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Problem 93: If $n$ distinct points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ form a convex polygon, then they
    51вҶ’determine at least $\lfloor n/2 \rfloor$ distinct distances. Proved by Altman [Al63].
    52вҶ’-/
    53вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 52]
    54вҶ’theorem erdos_93
    55вҶ’    (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)))
    56вҶ’    (hconv : InConvexPosition A)
    57вҶ’    (hn : 2 вүӨ A.card) :
    58вҶ’    A.card / 2 вүӨ (distinctDistances A).card := by
    59вҶ’  sorry
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’end Erdos93
    62вҶ’

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 660
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/660](https://www.erdosproblems.com/660)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Let $x_1, \ldots, x_n \in \mathbb{R}^3$ be the vertices of a convex polyhedron. Are there at
    25вҶ’least $(1 - o(1))n/2$ many distinct distances between the $x_i$?
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’For the similar problem in $\mathbb{R}^2$ there are always at least $n/2$ distances,
    28вҶ’as proved by Altman [Al63] (see [93]).
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’[Er97e] ErdЕ‘s, P., _Some of my favourite problems which recently have been solved_,
    31вҶ’Proc. Int. Conf. on Discrete Math. (1997), 527вҖ“533.
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’[Al63] Altman, E., _On a problem of P. ErdЕ‘s_, Amer. Math. Monthly 70 (1963), 148вҖ“157.
    34вҶ’-/
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’namespace Erdos660
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/-- Points in $\mathbb{R}^3$ are in convex position if every point is a vertex of the
    39вҶ’    convex hull, i.e., no point lies in the convex hull of the remaining points. -/
    40вҶ’def InConvexPosition3d (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))) : Prop :=
    41вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ P, p вҲү convexHull в„қ (вҶ‘(P.erase p) : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3)))
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’/-- The number of distinct pairwise distances determined by a finite point set in
    44вҶ’    $\mathbb{R}^3$. -/
    45вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistanceCount3d (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))) : в„• :=
    46вҶ’  Set.ncard {d : в„қ | вҲғ p вҲҲ P, вҲғ q вҲҲ P, p вү  q вҲ§ d = dist p q}
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’/--
    49вҶ’**ErdЕ‘s Problem 660** [Er97e, p.531]:
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’Let $x_1, \ldots, x_n \in \mathbb{R}^3$ be the vertices of a convex polyhedron. Then the number
    52вҶ’of distinct distances determined by these points is at least $(1 - o(1)) \cdot n/2$,
    53вҶ’i.e., for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists $N$ such that for all $n \geq N$, the number of
    54вҶ’distinct distances is at least $(1 - \varepsilon) \cdot n/2$.
    55вҶ’-/
    56вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 52]
    57вҶ’theorem erdos_660 :
    58вҶ’    answer(sorry) вҶ”
    59вҶ’    вҲҖ Оө : в„қ, Оө > 0 вҶ’
    60вҶ’      вҲғ N : в„•, вҲҖ n : в„•, n вүҘ N вҶ’
    61вҶ’        вҲҖ P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3)),
    62вҶ’          P.card = n вҶ’
    63вҶ’          InConvexPosition3d P вҶ’
    64вҶ’          (distinctDistanceCount3d P : в„қ) вүҘ (1 - Оө) * (n : в„қ) / 2 := by
    65вҶ’  sorry
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’end Erdos660
    68вҶ’

    16вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’/-!
    19вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 1082
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1082](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1082)
    22вҶ’-/
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’namespace Erdos1082
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’open EuclideanGeometry
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’/-- Given a finite set of points in the plane, we define the number of distinct distances between
    29вҶ’pairs of points.
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’TODO(csonne): Remove this once formal conjectures is bumped.
    32вҶ’-/
    33вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances (points : Finset в„қВІ) : в„• :=
    34вҶ’  (points.offDiag.image fun (pair : в„қВІ Г— в„қВІ) => dist pair.1 pair.2).card
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/-- The number of distinct distances from a finite set `points` to a point `pt`.
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’TODO(csonne): Move to ForMathlib.
    39вҶ’-/
    40вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistancesFrom (points : Finset в„қВІ) (pt : в„қВІ) : в„• :=
    41вҶ’  (points.image fun x => dist x pt).card
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’/--
    44вҶ’Let $A\subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of $n$ points with no three on a line.
    45вҶ’Does $A$ determine at least $\lfloor n/2\rfloor$ distinct distances?
    46вҶ’-/
    47вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 51]
    48вҶ’theorem erdos_1082.parts.i : answer(sorry) вҶ” вҲҖ (A : Finset в„қВІ) (hA_n3c : NonTrilinear (A : Set в„қВІ)),
    49вҶ’    A.card / 2 вүӨ distinctDistances A:= by
    50вҶ’  sorry
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’/--
    53вҶ’Let $A\subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of $n$ points with no three on a line.
    54вҶ’Must there exist a single point from which there are at least $\lfloor n/2\rfloor$ distinct
    55вҶ’distances?
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’This question has been answered negatively by Xichuan in the
    58вҶ’[comments](https://www.erdosproblems.com/forum/thread/1082), who gave a set of $42$ points in
    59вҶ’$\mathbb{R}^2$, with no three on a line, such that each point determines only $20$ distinct distances.
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’A smaller counterexample has been formalised here: it comprised of $8$ points, where each point only
    62вҶ’determines $3$ distances.
    63вҶ’-/
    64вҶ’@[category research formally solved using formal_conjectures at
    65вҶ’"https://github.com/google-deepmind/formal-conjectures/blob/0aca4d71095301c0fd2dca32611b7addb2ea735c/FormalConjectures/ErdosProblems/1082.lean", AMS 51]
    66вҶ’theorem erdos_1082.parts.ii : answer(False) вҶ”
    67вҶ’    вҲҖ (A : Finset в„қВІ) (hA : A.Nonempty) (hA_n3c : NonTrilinear (A : Set в„қВІ)),
    68вҶ’    вҲғ (a : в„қВІ) (ha : a вҲҲ A), A.card / 2 вүӨ distinctDistancesFrom A a - 1 := by
    69вҶ’  sorry
    70вҶ’end Erdos1082
    71вҶ’

    17вҶ’import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Orientation
    18вҶ’import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2
    19вҶ’import Mathlib.Geometry.Euclidean.Angle.Oriented.Affine
    20вҶ’import Mathlib.Geometry.Euclidean.Triangle

    22вҶ’import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Logic.Equiv.Fin.Rotate
    23вҶ’import FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Data.Set.Triplewise
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’scoped[EuclideanGeometry] notation "в„қВІ" => EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’open scoped EuclideanGeometry
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’/-- Oriented angles make sense in 2d.
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’Note: this can't blindly be added to mathlib as it creates an "instance diamond"
    32вҶ’with an instance for modules satisfying `is_empty`. -/
    33вҶ’noncomputable instance Module.orientedEuclideanSpaceFinTwo : Module.Oriented в„қ в„қВІ (Fin 2) :=
    34вҶ’  вҹЁBasis.orientation <| PiLp.basisFun 2 _ _вҹ©
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/-- Two dimensional euclidean space is two-dimensional. -/
    37вҶ’instance fact_finrank_euclideanSpace_fin_two : Fact (Module.finrank в„қ в„қВІ = 2) :=
    38вҶ’  вҹЁfinrank_euclideanSpace_finвҹ©
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’open scoped EuclideanGeometry
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’open scoped Real
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’namespace EuclideanGeometry
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’variable {V P : Type*} {n : в„•}
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’variable [NormedAddCommGroup V] [InnerProductSpace в„қ V] [MetricSpace P] [NormedAddTorsor V P]
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’variable [Module.Oriented в„қ V (Fin 2)] [Fact (Module.finrank в„қ V = 2)] {p : Fin n вҶ’ P}
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’/-- We say a subset `A` of points in the plane is non-trilinear
    53вҶ’if it contains no three points that lie on the same line. -/
    54вҶ’def NonTrilinear (A : Set P) : Prop :=
    55вҶ’  A.Triplewise (fun x y z вҶҰ В¬ Collinear в„қ {x, y, z})
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’/-- `ConvexIndep S` means that `S` consists of extremal points of its convex hull,
    58вҶ’i.e., the point set encloses a convex shape.
    59вҶ’Also known as a "convex-independent set". -/
    60вҶ’def ConvexIndep (S : Set в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    61вҶ’  вҲҖ a вҲҲ S, a вҲү convexHull в„қ (S \ {a})
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’/-- The set `P` contains a convex `n`-gon.
    64вҶ’See also `IsConvexPolygon`. -/
    65вҶ’def HasConvexNGon (n : в„•) (P : Set в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    66вҶ’  вҲғ S : Finset в„қВІ, S.card = n вҲ§ вҶ‘S вҠҶ P вҲ§ ConvexIndep S
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’/-- The statement that a sequence of points form a counter-clockwise convex polygon. -/
    69вҶ’def IsCcwConvexPolygon (p : Fin n вҶ’ P) : Prop :=
    70вҶ’  вҲҖ вҰғi j kвҰ„, i < j вҶ’ j < k вҶ’ (вҲЎ (p i) (p j) (p k)).sign = 1
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’theorem IsCcwConvexPolygon.sign_oangle (hp : IsCcwConvexPolygon p) {i j k : Fin n}
    73вҶ’  (hij : i < j) (hjk : j < k) : (вҲЎ (p i) (p j) (p k)).sign = 1 := hp hij hjk
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’set_option linter.docPrime false in
    76вҶ’theorem IsCcwConvexPolygon.sign_oangle' (hp : IsCcwConvexPolygon p) {i j k : Fin n}
    77вҶ’    (hij : i < j) (hjk : j < k) : (вҲЎ (p j) (p k) (p i)).sign = 1 := by
    78вҶ’  rw [EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rotate_sign]
    79вҶ’  exact hp hij hjk
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’set_option linter.docPrime false in
    82вҶ’theorem IsCcwConvexPolygon.sign_oangle'' (hp : IsCcwConvexPolygon p) {i j k : Fin n}
    83вҶ’    (hij : i < j) (hjk : j < k) : (вҲЎ (p k) (p i) (p j)).sign = 1 := by
    84вҶ’  rw [вҶҗ EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rotate_sign]
    85вҶ’  exact hp hij hjk
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’theorem IsCcwConvexPolygon.sign_oangle_finRotate (hp : IsCcwConvexPolygon p)
    88вҶ’    (hn : 3 вүӨ n) (i : Fin n) :
    89вҶ’    (вҲЎ (p i) (p <| finRotate _ i) (p <| finRotate _ (finRotate _ i))).sign = 1 := by
    90вҶ’  obtain вҹЁn, rflвҹ© := le_iff_exists_add'.mp hn
    91вҶ’  by_cases hi : i = Fin.last (n + 2)
    92вҶ’  В· rw [hi, finRotate_last, finRotate_apply_zero]
    93вҶ’    exact hp.sign_oangle'' Fin.zero_lt_one Fin.one_lt_last
    94вҶ’  by_cases hi' : finRotate _ i = Fin.last (n + 2)
    95вҶ’  В· rw [hi', finRotate_last]
    96вҶ’    refine hp.sign_oangle' ?_ ((Fin.le_last _).lt_of_ne hi)
    97вҶ’    rw [Fin.pos_iff_ne_zero]
    98вҶ’    rintro rfl
    99вҶ’    rw [finRotate_apply_zero] at hi'
   100вҶ’    exact Fin.one_lt_last.ne hi'
   101вҶ’  apply hp.sign_oangle <;> apply lt_finRotate_of_ne_last <;> assumption
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’@[simp] theorem isCcwConvexPolygon_zero (p : Fin 0 вҶ’ P) : IsCcwConvexPolygon p := finZeroElim
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’@[simp] theorem isCcwConvexPolygon_one (p : Fin 1 вҶ’ P) : IsCcwConvexPolygon p := by intro; omega
   106вҶ’
   107вҶ’@[simp] theorem isCcwConvexPolygon_two (p : Fin 2 вҶ’ P) : IsCcwConvexPolygon p := by intro; omega
   108вҶ’
   109вҶ’set_option linter.docPrime false in
   110вҶ’theorem isCcwConvexPolygon_four' {p : Fin 4 вҶ’ P} :
   111вҶ’    IsCcwConvexPolygon p вҶ” (вҲЎ (p 0) (p 1) (p 2)).sign = 1 вҲ§ (вҲЎ (p 1) (p 2) (p 3)).sign = 1 вҲ§
   112вҶ’    (вҲЎ (p 2) (p 3) (p 0)).sign = 1 вҲ§ (вҲЎ (p 3) (p 0) (p 1)).sign = 1 := by
   113вҶ’  refine вҹЁfun h вҶҰ ?_, fun вҹЁh1, h2, h3, h4вҹ© вҶҰ ?_вҹ©
   114вҶ’  В· obtain вҹЁh01, h12, h23вҹ© : (0 : Fin 4) < 1 вҲ§ (1 : Fin 4) < 2 вҲ§ (2 : Fin 4) < 3 := by simp
   115вҶ’    В· repeat' constructor
   116вҶ’      В· exact h.sign_oangle h01 h12
   117вҶ’      В· exact h.sign_oangle h12 h23
   118вҶ’      В· exact h.sign_oangle' (h01.trans h12) h23
   119вҶ’      В· exact h.sign_oangle'' h01 (h12.trans h23)
   120вҶ’  В· intro i j k hij hjk
   121вҶ’    fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> fin_cases k <;> simp at hij hjk
   122вҶ’    В· exact h1
   123вҶ’    В· rw [EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rotate_sign]
   124вҶ’      exact h4
   125вҶ’    В· rw [вҶҗ EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rotate_sign]
   126вҶ’      exact h3
   127вҶ’    В· exact h2
   128вҶ’
   129вҶ’@[simp]
   130вҶ’theorem isCcwConvexPolygon_four (A B C D : P) :
   131вҶ’    IsCcwConvexPolygon ![A, B, C, D] вҶ”
   132вҶ’      (вҲЎ A B C).sign = 1 вҲ§ (вҲЎ B C D).sign = 1 вҲ§ (вҲЎ C D A).sign = 1 вҲ§ (вҲЎ D A B).sign = 1 :=
   133вҶ’  isCcwConvexPolygon_four'
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’/-- The statement that a sequence of points form a convex polygon. -/
   136вҶ’def IsConvexPolygon {n : в„•} (p : Fin n вҶ’ P) : Prop :=
   137вҶ’  IsCcwConvexPolygon p вҲЁ IsCcwConvexPolygon fun i => p (-i)
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’/-- Three affine independent points always form a convex polygon. -/
   140вҶ’theorem isConvexPolygon_three_of_affineIndependent {A B C : P}
   141вҶ’    (hABC : AffineIndependent в„қ ![A, B, C]) : IsConvexPolygon ![A, B, C] := by
   142вҶ’  rw [вҶҗ oangle_ne_zero_and_ne_pi_iff_affineIndependent, вҶҗ Real.Angle.sign_ne_zero_iff] at hABC
   143вҶ’  cases hsABC : (вҲЎ A B C).sign
   144вҶ’  В· exact (hABC hsABC).elim
   145вҶ’  В· right
   146вҶ’    intro i j k hij hjk
   147вҶ’    fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> fin_cases k <;> simp at hij hjk
   148вҶ’    rw [EuclideanGeometry.oangle_rev, Real.Angle.sign_neg, neg_eq_iff_eq_neg]
   149вҶ’    exact (oangle_rotate_sign A B C).trans hsABC
   150вҶ’  В· left
   151вҶ’    intro i j k hij hjk
   152вҶ’    fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;> fin_cases k <;> simp at hij hjk
   153вҶ’    exact hsABC
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’theorem isConvexPolygon_three_iff_affineIndependent {A B C : P} :
   156вҶ’    IsConvexPolygon ![A, B, C] вҶ” AffineIndependent в„қ ![A, B, C] := by
   157вҶ’  refine вҹЁfun h => ?_, isConvexPolygon_three_of_affineIndependentвҹ©
   158вҶ’  rw [вҶҗ oangle_ne_zero_and_ne_pi_iff_affineIndependent, вҶҗ Real.Angle.sign_ne_zero_iff]
   159вҶ’  let p := ![A, B, C]
   160вҶ’  change IsConvexPolygon p at h
   161вҶ’  change Real.Angle.sign (вҲЎ (p 0) (p 1) (p 2)) вү  0
   162вҶ’  cases h with
   163вҶ’  | inl h =>
   164вҶ’    rw [h.sign_oangle (by simp) (by simp)]
   165вҶ’    rintro вҹЁвҹ©
   166вҶ’  | inr h =>
   167вҶ’    suffices Real.Angle.sign (вҲЎ (p 0) (p 2) (p 1)) = 1 by rw [вҶҗ oangle_swapвӮӮвӮғ_sign, this]; rintro вҹЁвҹ©
   168вҶ’    exact h.sign_oangle (i := 0) (j := 1) (k := 2) (by simp) (by simp)
   169вҶ’
   170вҶ’theorem isConvexPolygon_triangle (t : Affine.Triangle в„қ P) : IsConvexPolygon t.points := by
   171вҶ’  have : t.points = ![t.points 0, t.points 1, t.points 2] := by ext i; fin_cases i <;> rfl
   172вҶ’  rw [this, isConvexPolygon_three_iff_affineIndependent, вҶҗ this]
   173вҶ’  exact t.independent
   174вҶ’
   175вҶ’noncomputable def triangle_area (a b c : P) : в„қ :=
   176вҶ’  positiveOrientation.areaForm (a -бөҘ c) (b -бөҘ c) / 2
   177вҶ’
   178вҶ’lemma triangle_area_eq_det (a b c : в„қВІ) :
   179вҶ’    triangle_area a b c =
   180вҶ’    Matrix.det !![a 0, b 0, c 0;
   181вҶ’                  a 1, b 1, c 1;
   182вҶ’                  1,   1,   1] / 2 := by
   183вҶ’  rw [triangle_area, Orientation.areaForm_to_volumeForm,
   184вҶ’    positiveOrientation.volumeForm_robust (EuclideanSpace.basisFun (Fin 2) в„қ) rfl, Module.Basis.det_apply]
   185вҶ’  suffices (a 0 - c 0) * (b 1 - c 1) - (b 0 - c 0) * (a 1 - c 1) =
   186вҶ’      a 0 * b 1 - a 0 * c 1 - b 0 * a 1 + b 0 * c 1 + c 0 * a 1 - c 0 * b 1 by
   187вҶ’    simp [Matrix.det_fin_two, Matrix.det_fin_three, Module.Basis.toMatrix, this]
   188вҶ’  ring
   189вҶ’
   190вҶ’/--
   191вҶ’Given a finite set of points in the plane, we define the number of distinct distances between pairs
   192вҶ’of points.
   193вҶ’-/
   194вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances (points : Finset в„қВІ) : в„• :=
   195вҶ’  (points.offDiag.image fun (pair : в„қВІ Г— в„қВІ) => dist pair.1 pair.2).card
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’end EuclideanGeometry

   200вҶ’def IsIsosceles {Оұ : Type*} [Dist Оұ] (p q r : Оұ) : Prop :=
   201вҶ’  dist p q = dist q r вҲЁ dist q r = dist r p вҲЁ dist r p = dist p q
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’nonrec def Set.IsIsosceles {Оұ : Type} [Dist Оұ] (A : Set Оұ) :=
   204вҶ’  Nonempty A вҲ§ A.Triplewise (IsIsosceles В· В· В·)

    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 94
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/94](https://www.erdosproblems.com/94)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’For $n$ points in convex position with distance multiplicities $f(u_i)$, the sum
    25вҶ’$\sum_i f(u_i)^2$ is $O(n^3)$. Proved by Lefmann and Thiele.
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’[LeTh95] Lefmann, H. and Thiele, T., _Point sets with distinct distances_.
    28вҶ’Combinatorica 15 (1995), 379вҖ“408.
    29вҶ’-/
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’namespace Erdos94
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/--
    34вҶ’A finite set of points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ is in convex position if no point lies in
    35вҶ’the convex hull of the remaining points. Equivalently, all points are
    36вҶ’vertices of their convex hull (they form a convex polygon).
    37вҶ’-/
    38вҶ’def ConvexPosition (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    39вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ A, p вҲү convexHull в„қ ((A : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) \ {p})
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’/--
    42вҶ’The count of ordered quadruples $(a, b, c, d)$ from $A$ with $a \neq b$, $c \neq d$,
    43вҶ’and $\operatorname{dist}(a, b) = \operatorname{dist}(c, d)$. This equals
    44вҶ’$4 \cdot \sum_i f(u_i)^2$ where $f(u_i)$ counts unordered pairs at distance $u_i$.
    45вҶ’-/
    46вҶ’noncomputable def distSqSum (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : в„• :=
    47вҶ’  let E := EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)
    48вҶ’  Set.ncard {x : E Г— E Г— E Г— E |
    49вҶ’    x.1 вҲҲ (A : Set E) вҲ§ x.2.1 вҲҲ (A : Set E) вҲ§
    50вҶ’    x.2.2.1 вҲҲ (A : Set E) вҲ§ x.2.2.2 вҲҲ (A : Set E) вҲ§
    51вҶ’    x.1 вү  x.2.1 вҲ§ x.2.2.1 вү  x.2.2.2 вҲ§
    52вҶ’    dist x.1 x.2.1 = dist x.2.2.1 x.2.2.2}
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’/--
    55вҶ’Suppose $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are in convex position (they form the vertices of
    56вҶ’a convex polygon). Let $\{u_1, \ldots, u_t\}$ be the set of distinct pairwise
    57вҶ’distances, and let $f(u_i)$ denote the number of pairs at distance $u_i$. Then
    58вҶ’$$\sum_i f(u_i)^2 \ll n^3.$$
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’This was proved by Lefmann and Thiele [LeTh95] under the weaker
    61вҶ’assumption that no three points are collinear.
    62вҶ’-/
    63вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    64вҶ’theorem erdos_94 :
    65вҶ’    вҲғ C : в„қ, C > 0 вҲ§
    66вҶ’    вҲҖ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)),
    67вҶ’      ConvexPosition A вҶ’
    68вҶ’      (distSqSum A : в„қ) вүӨ C * (A.card : в„қ) ^ 3 := by
    69вҶ’  sorry
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’end Erdos94

    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 96
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/96](https://www.erdosproblems.com/96)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’ErdЕ‘sвҖ“Moser conjecture: if $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are in convex position, then the number
    25вҶ’of pairs at unit distance is $O(n)$.
    26вҶ’-/
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’namespace Erdos96
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’/--
    31вҶ’A finite set of points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ is in convex position if no point lies in the
    32вҶ’convex hull of the remaining points. Equivalently, the points are the vertices
    33вҶ’of a convex polygon.
    34вҶ’-/
    35вҶ’def ConvexPosition (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    36вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ P, p вҲү convexHull в„қ (вҶ‘(P.erase p) : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)))
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/--
    39вҶ’The number of ordered pairs of distinct points in $P$ that are at unit distance.
    40вҶ’(Using ordered pairs; the number of unordered pairs is exactly half this,
    41вҶ’so the $O(n)$ bound is equivalent.)
    42вҶ’-/
    43вҶ’noncomputable def unitDistancePairCount (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : в„• :=
    44вҶ’  (P.offDiag.filter (fun pq => dist pq.1 pq.2 = 1)).card
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’/--
    47вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Problem 96 (ErdЕ‘sвҖ“Moser conjecture):
    48вҶ’If $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ form a convex polygon (are in convex position), then there
    49вҶ’are $O(n)$ many pairs which are distance $1$ apart.
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’Formally: there exists an absolute constant $C > 0$ such that for every finite
    52вҶ’set $P$ of points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ in convex position, the number of (ordered) pairs at
    53вҶ’unit distance is at most $C \cdot |P|$.
    54вҶ’-/
    55вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 52]
    56вҶ’theorem erdos_96 :
    57вҶ’    вҲғ C : в„қ, C > 0 вҲ§
    58вҶ’    вҲҖ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
    59вҶ’      ConvexPosition P вҶ’
    60вҶ’      (unitDistancePairCount P : в„қ) вүӨ C * (P.card : в„қ) := by
    61вҶ’  sorry
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’end Erdos96
    64вҶ’

    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 97
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/97](https://www.erdosproblems.com/97)
    23вҶ’-/
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’open EuclideanGeometry
    26вҶ’open Real
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’namespace Erdos97
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’/--
    31вҶ’A set of points $A$ has n equidistant points at $p$
    32вҶ’if there exist at least $n$ other points in $A$ that are equidistant from $p$.
    33вҶ’-/
    34вҶ’def HasNEquidistantPointsAt (n : в„•) (A : Finset в„қВІ) (p : в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    35вҶ’  вҲғ r : в„қ, r > 0 вҲ§ (A.filter fun q вҶҰ dist p q = r).card вүҘ n
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’/--
    38вҶ’A set of points $A$ has n equidistant points on a set of points $B$
    39вҶ’if for every point in $B$, there exist at least $n$ other points in $A$ that are equidistant from it.
    40вҶ’-/
    41вҶ’def HasNEquidistantPointsOn (n : в„•) (A : Finset в„қВІ) (B : Finset в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    42вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ B, HasNEquidistantPointsAt n A p
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’/--
    45вҶ’A set of points $A$ has n equidistant property
    46вҶ’if for every point in $A$, there exist at least $n$ other points in $A$ that are equidistant from it.
    47вҶ’-/
    48вҶ’def HasNEquidistantProperty (n : в„•) (A : Finset в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    49вҶ’  HasNEquidistantPointsOn n A A
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’/--
    52вҶ’A set of points $A$ has n unit distance points at $p$
    53вҶ’if there exist at least $n$ other points in $A$ that are at unit distance from $p$.
    54вҶ’-/
    55вҶ’def HasNUnitDistancePointsAt (n : в„•) (A : Finset в„қВІ) (p : в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    56вҶ’  (A.filter fun q вҶҰ dist p q = 1).card вүҘ n
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’/--
    59вҶ’A set of points $A$ has n unit distance points on a set of points $B$
    60вҶ’if for every point in $B$, there exist at least $n$ other points in $A$ that are at unit distance from it.
    61вҶ’-/
    62вҶ’def HasNUnitDistancePointsOn (n : в„•) (A : Finset в„қВІ) (B : Finset в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    63вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ B, HasNUnitDistancePointsAt n A p
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’/--
    66вҶ’A set of points $A$ has n unit distance property
    67вҶ’if for every point in $A$, there exist at least $n$ other points in $A$ that are at unit distance from it.
    68вҶ’-/
    69вҶ’def HasNUnitDistanceProperty (n : в„•) (A : Finset в„қВІ) : Prop :=
    70вҶ’  HasNUnitDistancePointsOn n A A
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’/--
    73вҶ’Does every convex polygon have a vertex with no other 4 vertices equidistant from it?
    74вҶ’-/
    75вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 52]
    76вҶ’theorem erdos_97 :
    77вҶ’    answer(sorry) вҶ” вҲҖ A : Finset в„қВІ, A.Nonempty вҶ’ ConvexIndep A вҶ’ В¬HasNEquidistantProperty 4 A := by
    78вҶ’  sorry
    79вҶ’
    80вҶ’/--
    81вҶ’ErdЕ‘s originally conjectured this (in [Er46b]) with no 3 vertices equidistant,
    82вҶ’but Danzer found a convex polygon on 9 points such that every vertex has three
    83вҶ’vertices equidistant from it (but this distance depends on the vertex).
    84вҶ’Danzer's construction is explained in [Er87b].
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’[Er46b] ErdЕ‘s, P., _On sets of distances of $n$ points_. Amer. Math. Monthly (1946), 248-250.
    87вҶ’[Er87b] ErdЕ‘s, P., _Some combinatorial and metric problems in geometry_. Intuitive geometry (SiГіfok, 1985), 167-177.
    88вҶ’-/
    89вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 52]
    90вҶ’theorem erdos_97.variants.three_equidistant :
    91вҶ’    вҲғ A : Finset в„қВІ, A.Nonempty вҲ§ ConvexIndep A вҲ§ HasNEquidistantProperty 3 A := by
    92вҶ’  let AвӮҒ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(-вҲҡ3), -1]
    93вҶ’  let AвӮӮ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(вҲҡ3), -1]
    94вҶ’  let AвӮғ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[0, 2]
    95вҶ’  let BвӮҒ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(-8991 / 10927 * вҲҡ3), -26503 / 10927]
    96вҶ’  let BвӮӮ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(-17747 / 10947 * вҲҡ3), -235 / 10927]
    97вҶ’  let BвӮғ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(-8756 / 10927 * вҲҡ3), 26738 / 10927]
    98вҶ’  let CвӮҒ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(-10753 / 18529 * вҲҡ3), -44665 / 18529]
    99вҶ’  let CвӮӮ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(27709 / 18529 * вҲҡ3), 6203 / 18529]
   100вҶ’  let CвӮғ : в„қВІ := !вӮӮ[(-16956 / 18529 * вҲҡ3), 38462 / 18529]
   101вҶ’  use {AвӮҒ, AвӮӮ, AвӮғ, BвӮҒ, BвӮӮ, BвӮғ, CвӮҒ, CвӮӮ, CвӮғ}
   102вҶ’  sorry
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’/--
   105вҶ’ErdЕ‘s also conjectured that there is a $k$ for which every convex polygon has a vertex
   106вҶ’with no other $k$ vertices equidistant from it.
   107вҶ’-/
   108вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 52]
   109вҶ’theorem erdos_97.variants.k_equidistant : answer(sorry) вҶ”
   110вҶ’    вҲғ k : в„•, вҲҖ A : Finset в„қВІ, A.Nonempty вҶ’ ConvexIndep A вҶ’ В¬HasNEquidistantProperty k A := by
   111вҶ’  sorry
   112вҶ’
   113вҶ’/--
   114вҶ’Fishburn and Reeds [FiRe92] have found a convex polygon on 20 points such that
   115вҶ’every vertex has three vertices equidistant from it (and this distance is the same for all vertices).
   116вҶ’
   117вҶ’[FiRe92] Fishburn, P. C. and Reeds, J. A., _Unit distances between vertices of a convex polygon_. Comput. Geom. (1992), 81-91.
   118вҶ’-/
   119вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 52]
   120вҶ’theorem erdos_97.variants.three_unit_distance :
   121вҶ’    вҲғ A : Finset в„қВІ, A.Nonempty вҲ§ ConvexIndep A вҲ§ HasNUnitDistanceProperty 3 A := by
   122вҶ’  sorry
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’/--
   125вҶ’A two-part partition $\{A, B\}$ of $V$ is a cut if the convex hulls of $A$ and $B$ are disjoint.
   126вҶ’-/
   127вҶ’def IsCut (V A B : Finset в„қВІ) : Prop :=
   128вҶ’  A вҲӘ B = V вҲ§ Disjoint A B вҲ§
   129вҶ’  Disjoint (convexHull в„қ (A : Set в„қВІ)) (convexHull в„қ (B : Set в„қВІ))
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’/--
   132вҶ’Fishburn and Reeds [FiRe92] also proved that the smallest $n$ for which there exists
   133вҶ’a convex $n$-gon and a cut $\{A, B\}$ of its vertices such that $|\{b \in B : d(a, b) = 1\}| вүҘ 3$
   134вҶ’for all $a \in A$, and $|\{a \in A : d(a, b) = 1\}| вүҘ 3$ for all $b \in B$, is $n = 20$.
   135вҶ’-/
   136вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 52]
   137вҶ’theorem erdos_97.variants.three_unit_distance_cut_min :
   138вҶ’    sInf {n : в„• | вҲғ (V : Finset в„қВІ) (A B : Finset в„қВІ),
   139вҶ’      n = V.card вҲ§ ConvexIndep V вҲ§ A.Nonempty вҲ§ B.Nonempty вҲ§ IsCut V A B вҲ§
   140вҶ’      HasNUnitDistancePointsOn 3 B A вҲ§ HasNUnitDistancePointsOn 3 A B} = 20 := by
   141вҶ’  sorry
   142вҶ’
   143вҶ’end Erdos97
   144вҶ’

    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 651
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/651](https://www.erdosproblems.com/651)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Let $f_k(n)$ denote the smallest integer such that any $f_k(n)$ points in general
    25вҶ’position in $\mathbb{R}^k$ contain $n$ points forming the vertices of a convex polytope.
    26вҶ’ErdЕ‘s conjectured that $f_k(n)$ grows at least exponentially in $n$ for each fixed
    27вҶ’$k \geq 2$. This was disproved for $k \geq 3$ by Pohoata and Zakharov, who showed
    28вҶ’$f_3(n) \leq 2^{o(n)}$.
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’[Er97e] ErdЕ‘s, P., *Some of my favourite problems which recently have been solved*, 1997.
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’[PoZa22] Pohoata, C. and Zakharov, D., *Convex polytopes from fewer points*, 2022.
    33вҶ’-/
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’namespace Erdos651
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’/-- A finite point set in $\mathbb{R}^k$ is in general position if any $k+1$ distinct
    38вҶ’points are affinely independent (no $k+1$ points lie on a common hyperplane). -/
    39вҶ’def InGeneralPositionRk (k : в„•) (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k))) : Prop :=
    40вҶ’  вҲҖ f : Fin (k + 1) вҶ’ EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k),
    41вҶ’    (вҲҖ i, f i вҲҲ P) вҶ’ Function.Injective f вҶ’
    42вҶ’    AffineIndependent в„қ f
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’/-- A finite point set in $\mathbb{R}^k$ is in convex position if no point lies in the
    45вҶ’convex hull of the remaining points (the points are the vertices of a convex polytope). -/
    46вҶ’def InConvexPositionRk (k : в„•) (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k))) : Prop :=
    47вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ S, p вҲү convexHull в„қ (вҶ‘(S.erase p) : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k)))
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’/-- $f_k(n)$: the smallest integer $m$ such that any $m$ points in general position
    50вҶ’in $\mathbb{R}^k$ contain $n$ points in convex position (forming the vertices of a
    51вҶ’convex polytope). -/
    52вҶ’noncomputable def fk (k n : в„•) : в„• :=
    53вҶ’  sInf {m : в„• | вҲҖ P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k)),
    54вҶ’    P.card = m вҶ’
    55вҶ’    InGeneralPositionRk k P вҶ’
    56вҶ’    вҲғ Q : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k)),
    57вҶ’      Q вҠҶ P вҲ§ Q.card = n вҲ§ InConvexPositionRk k Q}
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’/--
    60вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Problem 651 [Er97e]:
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’Let $f_k(n)$ denote the smallest integer such that any $f_k(n)$ points in general
    63вҶ’position in $\mathbb{R}^k$ contain $n$ points forming the vertices of a convex polytope.
    64вҶ’The conjecture asks whether $f_k(n)$ grows at least exponentially in $n$ for
    65вҶ’each fixed $k \geq 2$, i.e., whether there exists $c_k > 0$ such that
    66вҶ’$$f_k(n) > (1 + c_k)^n.$$
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’DISPROVED for $k \geq 3$: Pohoata and Zakharov [PoZa22] proved $f_3(n) \leq 2^{o(n)}$.
    69вҶ’-/
    70вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    71вҶ’theorem erdos_651 : answer(False) вҶ”
    72вҶ’    вҲҖ (k : в„•), k вүҘ 2 вҶ’ вҲғ c : в„қ, c > 0 вҲ§ вҲғ NвӮҖ : в„•, вҲҖ n : в„•, n вүҘ NвӮҖ вҶ’
    73вҶ’      (fk k n : в„қ) > (1 + c) ^ n := by
    74вҶ’  sorry
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’end Erdos651
    77вҶ’

    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 838
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’Let $f(n)$ be the minimum number of convex subsets determined by $n$ points in general position
    23вҶ’in $\mathbb{R}^2$. Does $\log f(n) / (\log n)^2$ converge to a constant?
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/838](https://www.erdosproblems.com/838)
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’[Er78c] ErdЕ‘s, P. (1978).
    28вҶ’-/
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’open Classical Filter Finset
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’open scoped Topology Real
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’namespace Erdos838
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/--
    37вҶ’A finite point set $P$ in $\mathbb{R}^2$ is in general position if no three of its points
    38вҶ’are collinear.
    39вҶ’-/
    40вҶ’def InGeneralPosition (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    41вҶ’  вҲҖ S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)),
    42вҶ’    S вҠҶ P вҶ’ S.card = 3 вҶ’ В¬Collinear в„қ (S : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)))
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’/--
    45вҶ’A finite point set $S$ in $\mathbb{R}^2$ is in convex position if no point of $S$ lies in
    46вҶ’the convex hull of the remaining points.
    47вҶ’-/
    48вҶ’def InConvexPosition (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    49вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ S, p вҲү convexHull в„қ (вҶ‘(S.erase p) : Set (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)))
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’/--
    52вҶ’The number of subsets of $P$ that are in convex position.
    53вҶ’-/
    54вҶ’noncomputable def numConvexSubsets (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : в„• :=
    55вҶ’  (P.powerset.filter fun S => InConvexPosition S).card
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’/--
    58вҶ’$f(n)$ is the minimum number of convex subsets determined by any $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$
    59вҶ’in general position (no three collinear).
    60вҶ’-/
    61вҶ’noncomputable def f (n : в„•) : в„• :=
    62вҶ’  sInf {k : в„• | вҲғ P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)),
    63вҶ’    P.card = n вҲ§ InGeneralPosition P вҲ§ numConvexSubsets P = k}
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’/--
    66вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Problem 838 (ErdЕ‘sвҖ“Hammer):
    67вҶ’Let $f(n)$ be the maximum number such that any $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$, with no three
    68вҶ’collinear, determine at least $f(n)$ different convex subsets. Does there exist
    69вҶ’a constant $c$ such that
    70вҶ’$$\lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{\log f(n)}{(\log n)^2} = c?$$
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’ErdЕ‘s proved that $n^{c_1 \log n} < f(n) < n^{c_2 \log n}$ for some constants
    73вҶ’$c_1, c_2 > 0$ [Er78c].
    74вҶ’-/
    75вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 5 52]
    76вҶ’theorem erdos_838 : answer(sorry) вҶ”
    77вҶ’    вҲғ c : в„қ, Tendsto (fun n : в„• => Real.log (f n : в„қ) / (Real.log (n : в„қ)) ^ 2)
    78вҶ’      atTop (nhds c) := by
    79вҶ’  sorry
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’end Erdos838
    82вҶ’

    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 95
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/95](https://www.erdosproblems.com/95)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’For $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ with distance multiplicities $f(u_i)$, the sum
    25вҶ’$\sum_i f(u_i)^2 \ll_\varepsilon n^{3+\varepsilon}$ for every $\varepsilon > 0$.
    26вҶ’Proved by Guth and Katz (2015) with the stronger bound $O(n^3 \log n)$.
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’[GuKa15] Guth, L. and Katz, N.H., _On the ErdЕ‘s distinct distances problem in the plane_,
    29вҶ’Annals of Mathematics **181** (2015), 155вҖ“190.
    30вҶ’-/
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’open Classical
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’namespace Erdos95
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/--
    37вҶ’The set of distinct distances determined by a finite point set $P$ in $\mathbb{R}^2$.
    38вҶ’-/
    39вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Finset в„қ :=
    40вҶ’  ((P.product P).filter (fun pq => pq.1 вү  pq.2)).image (fun pq => dist pq.1 pq.2)
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’/--
    43вҶ’The number of ordered pairs $(p, q)$ from $P$ with $p \neq q$ and $\operatorname{dist}(p, q) = d$.
    44вҶ’-/
    45вҶ’noncomputable def distMultiplicity (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) (d : в„қ) : в„• :=
    46вҶ’  ((P.product P).filter (fun pq => pq.1 вү  pq.2 вҲ§ dist pq.1 pq.2 = d)).card
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’/--
    49вҶ’Let $x_1, \ldots, x_n \in \mathbb{R}^2$ determine the set of distances $\{u_1, \ldots, u_t\}$.
    50вҶ’Suppose $u_i$ appears as the distance between $f(u_i)$ many pairs of points. Then for all
    51вҶ’$\varepsilon > 0$,
    52вҶ’$$\sum_i f(u_i)^2 \ll_\varepsilon n^{3+\varepsilon}.$$
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’This was proved by Guth and Katz (2015) who showed the stronger bound
    55вҶ’$\sum_i f(u_i)^2 \ll n^3 \log n$.
    56вҶ’-/
    57вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    58вҶ’theorem erdos_95 :
    59вҶ’    вҲҖ Оө : в„қ, Оө > 0 вҶ’
    60вҶ’      вҲғ C : в„қ, C > 0 вҲ§
    61вҶ’        вҲҖ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
    62вҶ’          ((distinctDistances P).sum
    63вҶ’            (fun d => (distMultiplicity P d) ^ 2) : в„қ) вүӨ
    64вҶ’            C * (P.card : в„қ) ^ ((3 : в„қ) + Оө) := by
    65вҶ’  sorry
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’end Erdos95
    68вҶ’

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 959
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/959](https://www.erdosproblems.com/959)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Let $A \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of size $n$ and let $\{d_1, \ldots, d_k\}$
    25вҶ’be the set of distinct distances determined by $A$. Let $f(d)$ be the number of
    26вҶ’times the distance $d$ is determined (as an unordered pair), and suppose the $d_i$
    27вҶ’are ordered such that $f(d_1) \geq f(d_2) \geq \cdots \geq f(d_k)$.
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’Estimate $\max (f(d_1) - f(d_2))$, where the maximum is taken over all $A$ of
    30вҶ’size $n$.
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’[Er84d] ErdЕ‘s, P., *Some old and new problems on combinatorial geometry*.
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’[CDL25] Clemen, F., Dumitrescu, A., and Liu, J., *On the gap between the most frequent and
    35вҶ’second most frequent distances*.
    36вҶ’-/
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’open Classical
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’namespace Erdos959
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’/-- The set of distinct distances determined by a finite point set in $\mathbb{R}^2$. -/
    43вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Finset в„қ :=
    44вҶ’  ((A Г—Лў A).filter (fun p => p.1 вү  p.2)).image (fun p => dist p.1 p.2)
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’/-- The multiplicity of distance $d$ in $A$: the number of unordered pairs at distance $d$. -/
    47вҶ’noncomputable def distMultiplicity (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) (d : в„қ) : в„• :=
    48вҶ’  ((A Г—Лў A).filter (fun p => p.1 вү  p.2 вҲ§ dist p.1 p.2 = d)).card / 2
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’/--
    51вҶ’**ErdЕ‘s Problem 959** [Er84d]:
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’For any sufficiently large $n$, there exists a set $A$ of $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$
    54вҶ’such that the gap between the most frequent and every other distance multiplicity
    55вҶ’is at least $c \cdot n \cdot \log n$ for some absolute constant $c > 0$.
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’Equivalently, $\max_A (f(d_1) - f(d_2)) \gg n \log n$ where $f(d_1) \geq f(d_2)$
    58вҶ’are the two largest distance multiplicities.
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’This lower bound was proved by Clemen, Dumitrescu, and Liu [CDL25].
    61вҶ’-/
    62вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 52]
    63вҶ’theorem erdos_959 :
    64вҶ’    вҲғ c : в„қ, c > 0 вҲ§ вҲғ N : в„•, вҲҖ n : в„•, n вүҘ N вҶ’
    65вҶ’      вҲғ (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))),
    66вҶ’        A.card = n вҲ§
    67вҶ’        вҲғ dвӮҒ вҲҲ distinctDistances A,
    68вҶ’          (вҲҖ d вҲҲ distinctDistances A, distMultiplicity A d вүӨ distMultiplicity A dвӮҒ) вҲ§
    69вҶ’          вҲҖ dвӮӮ вҲҲ distinctDistances A, dвӮӮ вү  dвӮҒ вҶ’
    70вҶ’            (distMultiplicity A dвӮҒ : в„қ) - (distMultiplicity A dвӮӮ : в„қ) вүҘ
    71вҶ’              c * (n : в„қ) * Real.log (n : в„қ) := by
    72вҶ’  sorry
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’end Erdos959
    75вҶ’

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 958
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/958](https://www.erdosproblems.com/958)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Let $A \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a finite set of size $n$, and let $\{d_1, \ldots, d_k\}$
    25вҶ’be the set of distinct distances determined by $A$. Let $f(d)$ be the multiplicity of $d$,
    26вҶ’the number of unordered pairs from $A$ at distance $d$ apart.
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’Is it true that $k = n - 1$ and $\{f(d_i)\} = \{1, \ldots, n-1\}$ if and only if $A$ is
    29вҶ’a set of equidistant points on a line or a circle?
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’ErdЕ‘s conjectured that the answer is no, and other such configurations exist [Er84c].
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’This was proved by Clemen, Dumitrescu, and Liu [CDL25], who observed that equidistant
    34вҶ’points on a short circular arc on a circle of radius 1, together with the centre, provide
    35вҶ’a counterexample.
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’[Er84c] ErdЕ‘s, P., _Some old and new problems on combinatorial geometry_, 1984.
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’[CDL25] Clemen, F., Dumitrescu, A., and Liu, Y., 2025.
    40вҶ’-/
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’namespace Erdos958
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’/-- The set of distinct distances determined by a finite point set in $\mathbb{R}^2$. -/
    45вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Finset в„қ :=
    46вҶ’  ((A Г—Лў A).filter (fun p => p.1 вү  p.2)).image (fun p => dist p.1 p.2)
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’/-- The number of unordered pairs in $A$ at distance $d$. -/
    49вҶ’noncomputable def distMultiplicity (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) (d : в„қ) : в„• :=
    50вҶ’  ((A Г—Лў A).filter (fun p => p.1 вү  p.2 вҲ§ dist p.1 p.2 = d)).card / 2
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’/-- The distance multiplicity property: $A$ has exactly $n-1$ distinct distances and the
    53вҶ’multiplicities are exactly $\{1, 2, \ldots, n-1\}$. -/
    54вҶ’def HasDistanceMultiplicityProperty (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    55вҶ’  A.card вүҘ 2 вҲ§
    56вҶ’  (distinctDistances A).card = A.card - 1 вҲ§
    57вҶ’  вҲҖ m : в„•, (1 вүӨ m вҲ§ m вүӨ A.card - 1) вҶ”
    58вҶ’    (вҲғ d вҲҲ distinctDistances A, distMultiplicity A d = m)
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’/-- A finite point set is equidistant-collinear if the points are equally spaced along a
    61вҶ’line: $A = \{p, p + v, p + 2v, \ldots, p + (n-1)v\}$ for some $p, v$ with $v \neq 0$. -/
    62вҶ’def IsEquidistantCollinear (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    63вҶ’  вҲғ (p v : EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)), v вү  0 вҲ§
    64вҶ’    вҲҖ a вҲҲ A, вҲғ i : Fin A.card, a = p + (i.val : в„қ) вҖў v
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’/-- A finite point set is equidistant-circular if the points are equally spaced on a
    67вҶ’circle: all points lie on a circle and consecutive points in a cyclic ordering have
    68вҶ’equal Euclidean distance. -/
    69вҶ’def IsEquidistantCircular (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    70вҶ’  A.card вүҘ 3 вҲ§
    71вҶ’  вҲғ (c : EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)) (r : в„қ), r > 0 вҲ§
    72вҶ’    (вҲҖ a вҲҲ A, dist a c = r) вҲ§
    73вҶ’    вҲғ (Пғ : в„• вҶ’ EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)),
    74вҶ’      (вҲҖ i, i < A.card вҶ’ Пғ i вҲҲ A) вҲ§
    75вҶ’      (вҲҖ a вҲҲ A, вҲғ i, i < A.card вҲ§ Пғ i = a) вҲ§
    76вҶ’      (вҲҖ i j, i < A.card вҶ’ j < A.card вҶ’ Пғ i = Пғ j вҶ’ i = j) вҲ§
    77вҶ’      вҲҖ i, i < A.card вҶ’
    78вҶ’        dist (Пғ i) (Пғ ((i + 1) % A.card)) = dist (Пғ 0) (Пғ 1)
    79вҶ’
    80вҶ’/--
    81вҶ’**ErdЕ‘s Problem 958** [Er84c]:
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’Is it true that if a finite set $A \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ satisfies the distance multiplicity
    84вҶ’property (exactly $n-1$ distinct distances with multiplicities $\{1, \ldots, n-1\}$), then $A$
    85вҶ’must be a set of equidistant points on a line or equidistant points on a circle?
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’The answer is no. This was proved by Clemen, Dumitrescu, and Liu [CDL25].
    88вҶ’-/
    89вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    90вҶ’theorem erdos_958 : answer(False) вҶ”
    91вҶ’    (вҲҖ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)),
    92вҶ’      HasDistanceMultiplicityProperty A вҶ’
    93вҶ’      (IsEquidistantCollinear A вҲЁ IsEquidistantCircular A)) := by
    94вҶ’  sorry
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’end Erdos958
    97вҶ’

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 653
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’For $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$, let $R(x_i)$ count the number of distinct distances from
    23вҶ’$x_i$ to all other points. Let $g(n)$ be the maximum number of distinct values the $R(x_i)$
    24вҶ’can take. Is it true that $g(n) \geq (1 - o(1))n$?
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/653](https://www.erdosproblems.com/653)
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’[Er97e] ErdЕ‘s, P., *Some of my favourite problems which recently have been solved*.
    29вҶ’-/
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’open Finset Classical
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’namespace Erdos653
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’/--
    36вҶ’For a configuration of $n$ points in the Euclidean plane, $R(x_i)$ counts the number
    37вҶ’of distinct distances from $x_i$ to all other points.
    38вҶ’-/
    39вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances (n : в„•) (x : Fin n вҶ’ EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))
    40вҶ’    (i : Fin n) : в„• :=
    41вҶ’  ((Finset.univ.filter (В· вү  i)).image (fun j => dist (x i) (x j))).card
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’/--
    44вҶ’For a configuration of $n$ points in the Euclidean plane, the number of distinct
    45вҶ’values that $R(x_i)$ takes over all $i$.
    46вҶ’-/
    47вҶ’noncomputable def numDistinctR (n : в„•) (x : Fin n вҶ’ EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)) : в„• :=
    48вҶ’  (Finset.univ.image (fun i => distinctDistances n x i)).card
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’/--
    51вҶ’Let $x_1, \ldots, x_n \in \mathbb{R}^2$ and
    52вҶ’$R(x_i) = \#\{|x_j - x_i| : j \neq i\}$. Order the points so that
    53вҶ’$R(x_1) \leq \cdots \leq R(x_n)$. Let $g(n)$ be the maximum number of distinct
    54вҶ’values the $R(x_i)$ can take. Is it true that $g(n) \geq (1 - o(1))n$? [Er97e]
    55вҶ’-/
    56вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 52]
    57вҶ’theorem erdos_653 :
    58вҶ’    answer(sorry) вҶ”
    59вҶ’      вҲҖ Оө : в„қ, Оө > 0 вҶ’
    60вҶ’        вҲғ N : в„•, вҲҖ n : в„•, N вүӨ n вҶ’
    61вҶ’          вҲғ x : Fin n вҶ’ EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2),
    62вҶ’            (numDistinctR n x : в„қ) вүҘ (1 - Оө) * n := by
    63вҶ’  sorry
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’end Erdos653
    66вҶ’

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 1089
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1089](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1089)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Let $g_d(n)$ be minimal such that every collection of $g_d(n)$ points in $\mathbb{R}^d$
    25вҶ’determines at least $n$ distinct distances. Does $\lim_{d \to \infty} g_d(n) / d^{n-1}$
    26вҶ’exist for all $n \geq 2$? The answer is yes, and the limit equals $1/(n-1)!$.
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’[Fe26] Aletheia-Zomlefer, established the lower bound
    29вҶ’$\binom{d+1}{n-1} + 1 \leq g_d(n)$.
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’[BBS83] Bannai, E., Bannai, E., and Stanton, D., established the upper bound
    32вҶ’$g_d(n) \leq \binom{d+n-1}{n-1} + 1$.
    33вҶ’-/
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’open Classical Finset Filter
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’namespace Erdos1089
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’/-- The set of distinct distances determined by a finite set of points
    40вҶ’in $d$-dimensional Euclidean space. -/
    41вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistances {d : в„•} (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin d))) : Finset в„қ :=
    42вҶ’  S.biUnion (fun a => (S.erase a).image (fun b => dist a b))
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’/-- The number of distinct distances determined by a finite set of points. -/
    45вҶ’noncomputable def distinctDistanceCount {d : в„•} (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin d))) : в„• :=
    46вҶ’  (distinctDistances S).card
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’/-- $g_d(n)$ is the minimal number of points in $\mathbb{R}^d$ such that any collection of
    49вҶ’that many points determines at least $n$ distinct distances. -/
    50вҶ’noncomputable def g (d n : в„•) : в„• :=
    51вҶ’  sInf {m : в„• | вҲҖ (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin d))), S.card вүҘ m вҶ’
    52вҶ’    distinctDistanceCount S вүҘ n}
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’/--
    55вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Problem 1089 (Kelly's question, resolved):
    56вҶ’Let $g_d(n)$ be minimal such that every collection of $g_d(n)$ points in $\mathbb{R}^d$ determines
    57вҶ’at least $n$ distinct distances. The limit $\lim_{d \to \infty} g_d(n) / d^{n-1}$ exists and
    58вҶ’equals $1/(n-1)!$ for all $n \geq 2$.
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’The lower bound $\binom{d+1}{n-1} + 1 \leq g_d(n)$ is due to Aletheia-Zomlefer [Fe26]
    61вҶ’and the upper bound $g_d(n) \leq \binom{d+n-1}{n-1} + 1$ is due to Bannai, Bannai,
    62вҶ’and Stanton [BBS83].
    63вҶ’-/
    64вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    65вҶ’theorem erdos_1089 :
    66вҶ’    answer(True) вҶ”
    67вҶ’      вҲҖ (n : в„•), 2 вүӨ n вҶ’
    68вҶ’        Tendsto (fun d : в„• => (g d n : в„қ) / (d : в„қ) ^ (n - 1))
    69вҶ’          atTop (nhds (1 / (Nat.factorial (n - 1) : в„қ))) := by
    70вҶ’  sorry
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’end Erdos1089
    73вҶ’

    15вҶ’-/
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 89
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/89](https://www.erdosproblems.com/89)
    23вҶ’-/
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’open Filter
    26вҶ’open EuclideanGeometry
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’namespace Erdos89
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’/--
    31вҶ’The minimum number of distinct distances guaranteed for any set of $n$ points.
    32вҶ’-/
    33вҶ’noncomputable def minimalDistinctDistances (n : в„•) : в„• :=
    34вҶ’  sInf {(distinctDistances points : в„қ) | (points : Finset в„қВІ) (_ : points.card = n)}
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’/--
    37вҶ’Does every set of $n$ distinct points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ determine $\gg \frac{n}{\sqrt{\log n}}$
    38вҶ’many distinct distances?
    39вҶ’-/
    40вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 52]
    41вҶ’theorem erdos_89 :
    42вҶ’    (fun (n : в„•) => n/(n : в„қ).log.sqrt) =O[atTop] (fun n => (minimalDistinctDistances n : в„қ)) := by
    43вҶ’  sorry
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’/--
    46вҶ’Guth and Katz [GuKa15] proved that there are always $\gg \frac{n}{\log n}$ many distinct distances.
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’[GuKa15] Guth, Larry and Katz, Nets Hawk, On the ErdЕ‘s distinct distances problem in the plane. Ann. of Math. (2) (2015), 155-190.
    49вҶ’-/
    50вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 52]
    51вҶ’theorem erdos_89.variants.n_dvd_log_n :
    52вҶ’    (fun (n : в„•) => n/(n : в„қ).log) =O[atTop] (fun n => (minimalDistinctDistances n : в„қ)) := by
    53вҶ’  sorry
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’/--
    56вҶ’This theorem provides a sanity check, showing that the main conjecture (`erdos_89`) is strictly
    57вҶ’stronger than the solved Guth and Katz result. It proves that, trivially, if the lower bound
    58вҶ’$\frac{n}{\sqrt{\log n}}$ holds, then the weaker lower bound $\frac{n}{\log n}$ must also hold.
    59вҶ’-/
    60вҶ’@[category test, AMS 52]
    61вҶ’theorem erdos_89.variants.implies_n_dvd_log_n (h : type_of% erdos_89) :
    62вҶ’    type_of% erdos_89.variants.n_dvd_log_n := by
    63вҶ’  refine .trans ?_ h
    64вҶ’  have := (Asymptotics.isLittleO_one_left_iff в„қ).mpr <| tendsto_norm_atTop_atTop.comp <|
    65вҶ’    (tendsto_rpow_atTop (show 0 < 1/2 by norm_num)).comp
    66вҶ’    (Real.tendsto_log_atTop.comp tendsto_natCast_atTop_atTop)
    67вҶ’  convert (Asymptotics.isBigO_refl (fun n : в„• вҶҰ n/(n : в„қ).log) _).mul this.isBigO using 1
    68вҶ’  В· simp
    69вҶ’  В· simp_rw [Function.comp, div_mul, вҶҗ Real.sqrt_eq_rpow, Real.div_sqrt]
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’-- TODO(firsching): formalize the rest of the remarks
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’end Erdos89
    75вҶ’

    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 659
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*References:*
    23вҶ’- [erdosproblems.com/659](https://www.erdosproblems.com/659)
    24вҶ’- [MoOs06] Moree, Pieter and Osburn, Robert, Two-dimensional lattices with few distances. Enseign. Math. (2) (2006), 361--380
    25вҶ’- [ErFi96] ErdЕ‘s, Paul and Fishburn, Peter, Maximum planar sets that determine {$k$} distances. Discrete Math. (1996), 115--125.
    26вҶ’- [Gr26](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.09102): Benjamin Grayzel, Solution to a Problem of ErdЕ‘s Concerning Distances and Points
    27вҶ’-/
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’open EuclideanGeometry Finset Real
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’namespace Erdos659
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/--
    34вҶ’Is there a set of $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ such that every subset of $4$ points determines at
    35вҶ’least $3$ distances, yet the total number of distinct distances is $\ll \frac{n}{\sqrt{\log n}}$?
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’There does exist such a set: a suitable truncation of the lattice
    38вҶ’$\{(a,b\sqrt{2}): a,b\in\mathbb{Z}\}$ suffices. This construction appears to have been first
    39вҶ’considered by Moree and Osburn \cite{MoOs06}, who proved that it has
    40вҶ’ $\ll \frac{n}{\sqrt{\log n}}$ many distinct distances. This construction was independently found by
    41вҶ’ [Lund and Sheffer](https://adamsheffer.wordpress.com/2014/07/16/point-sets-with-few-distinct-distances/),
    42вҶ’ who further noted that this configuration contains no squares or equilateral triangles.
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’There are only six possible configurations of $4$ points which determine only $2$ distances
    45вҶ’(first noted by ErdЕ‘s and Fishburn [ErFi96]), and five of them contain either a square or an
    46вҶ’equilateral triangle. The remaining configuration contains four points from a regular pentagon,
    47вҶ’and Grayzel [Gr26] (using Gemini) has noted in the comments that this configuration can also be
    48вҶ’ruled out, thus giving a complete solution to this problem. Boris Alexeev using Aristotle provides
    49вҶ’a formalisation of the proof.
    50вҶ’-/
    51вҶ’@[category research formally solved using lean4 at "https://github.com/plby/lean-proofs/blob/226d5fad7143dcebea2bbb5ec87f18a3a1dcea69/src/v4.24.0/ErdosProblems/Erdos659.lean", AMS 52]
    52вҶ’theorem erdos_659 : answer(True) вҶ” вҲғ A : в„• вҶ’ Finset в„қВІ,
    53вҶ’   (вҲҖ n, #(A n) = n вҲ§ вҲҖ S вҠҶ A n, #S = 4 вҶ’ 3 вүӨ distinctDistances S) вҲ§
    54вҶ’    (fun n вҶҰ distinctDistances (A n)) вүӘ fun n вҶҰ n / sqrt (log n) := by
    55вҶ’  sorry
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’end Erdos659
    58вҶ’

    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 216
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/216](https://www.erdosproblems.com/216)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Does $g(k)$ exist for all $k \geq 3$, where $g(k)$ is the smallest $N$ such that any set of $N$
    25вҶ’points in general position in $\mathbb{R}^2$ contains an empty convex $k$-gon?
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’[Ha78] Harborth, H., _Konvexe FГјnfecke in ebenen Punktmengen_. Elem. Math. 33 (1978), 116-118.
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’[Ho83] Horton, J. D., _Sets with no empty convex 7-gons_. Canad. Math. Bull. 26 (1983), 482-484.
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’[Ni07] NicolГЎs, C. M., _The empty hexagon theorem_. Discrete Comput. Geom. 38 (2007), 389-397.
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’[Ge08] Gerken, T., _Empty convex hexagons in planar point sets_. Discrete Comput. Geom. 39 (2008),
    34вҶ’239-272.
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’[HeSc24] Heule, M. J. H. and Scheucher, M., _Happy ending: An empty hexagon in every set of 30
    37вҶ’points_. J. ACM 71 (2024).
    38вҶ’-/
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’open Set
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’namespace Erdos216
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’/-- A point in the Euclidean plane $\mathbb{R}^2$. -/
    45вҶ’abbrev Point := EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’/--
    48вҶ’A finite set of points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ is in general position if no three distinct
    49вҶ’points are collinear (i.e., for any three distinct points, the third does
    50вҶ’not lie on the affine line through the other two).
    51вҶ’-/
    52вҶ’def InGeneralPosition (S : Finset Point) : Prop :=
    53вҶ’  вҲҖ pвӮҒ вҲҲ S, вҲҖ pвӮӮ вҲҲ S, вҲҖ pвӮғ вҲҲ S,
    54вҶ’    pвӮҒ вү  pвӮӮ вҶ’ pвӮҒ вү  pвӮғ вҶ’ pвӮӮ вү  pвӮғ вҶ’
    55вҶ’    pвӮғ вҲү (affineSpan в„қ ({pвӮҒ, pвӮӮ} : Set Point) : Set Point)
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’/--
    58вҶ’A finite set of points is in convex position if no point lies in the
    59вҶ’convex hull of the remaining points.
    60вҶ’-/
    61вҶ’def InConvexPosition (S : Finset Point) : Prop :=
    62вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ S, p вҲү convexHull в„қ (вҶ‘(S.erase p) : Set Point)
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’/--
    65вҶ’A point set $S$ contains an empty convex $k$-gon if there exists a subset $T \subseteq S$
    66вҶ’of exactly $k$ points such that:
    67вҶ’1. $T$ is in convex position (the $k$ points form the vertices of a convex polygon), and
    68вҶ’2. no other point of $S$ lies in the interior of the convex hull of $T$.
    69вҶ’-/
    70вҶ’def HasEmptyConvexKGon (S : Finset Point) (k : в„•) : Prop :=
    71вҶ’  вҲғ T : Finset Point, T вҠҶ S вҲ§ T.card = k вҲ§
    72вҶ’    InConvexPosition T вҲ§
    73вҶ’    вҲҖ p вҲҲ S, p вҲү T вҶ’ p вҲү interior (convexHull в„қ (вҶ‘T : Set Point))
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’/--
    76вҶ’Let $g(k)$ be the smallest integer $N$ (if it exists) such that any set of $N$
    77вҶ’points in general position in $\mathbb{R}^2$ contains an empty convex $k$-gon (a convex
    78вҶ’$k$-gon with no point of the set in its interior). Does $g(k)$ exist for
    79вҶ’all $k \geq 3$?
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’Known results:
    82вҶ’- $g(3) = 3$ (trivial) and $g(4) = 5$ (ErdЕ‘s).
    83вҶ’- $g(5) = 10$ (Harborth [Ha78]).
    84вҶ’- $g(6)$ exists (NicolГЎs [Ni07], Gerken [Ge08]), and $g(6) = 30$
    85вҶ’  (HeuleвҖ“Scheucher [HeSc24]).
    86вҶ’- DISPROVED for $k \geq 7$: Horton [Ho83] constructed arbitrarily large point
    87вҶ’  sets in general position with no empty convex $7$-gon, so $g(k)$ does not
    88вҶ’  exist for $k \geq 7$.
    89вҶ’-/
    90вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 52]
    91вҶ’theorem erdos_216 : answer(False) вҶ”
    92вҶ’    вҲҖ k : в„•, 3 вүӨ k вҶ’
    93вҶ’      вҲғ N : в„•, вҲҖ (S : Finset Point),
    94вҶ’        InGeneralPosition S вҶ’ N вүӨ S.card вҶ’
    95вҶ’        HasEmptyConvexKGon S k := by
    96вҶ’  sorry
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’end Erdos216
    99вҶ’

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 90: The unit distance problem
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/90](https://www.erdosproblems.com/90)
    23вҶ’-/
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’open Filter
    26вҶ’open scoped EuclideanGeometry
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’namespace Erdos90
    29вҶ’open Finset
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’/--
    32вҶ’Given a finite set of points, this function counts the number of **unordered pairs** of distinct
    33вҶ’points that are at a distance of exactly 1 from each other.
    34вҶ’-/
    35вҶ’noncomputable def unitDistancePairsCount (points : Finset в„қВІ) : в„• :=
    36вҶ’  (points.offDiag.filter (fun p => dist p.1 p.2 = 1)).card / 2
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’/--
    40вҶ’The set of all possible numbers of unit distances for a configuration of $n$ points.
    41вҶ’-/
    42вҶ’noncomputable def unitDistanceCounts (n : в„•) : Set в„• :=
    43вҶ’  {unitDistancePairsCount points | (points : Finset в„қВІ) (_ : points.card = n)}
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’/--
    46вҶ’This lemma confirms that the set of possible unit distance counts is bounded above, which
    47вҶ’ensures that taking the supremum (`sSup`) is a well-defined operation. The trivial upper bound is
    48вҶ’the total number of pairs of points, $\binom{n}{2}$.
    49вҶ’-/
    50вҶ’@[category test, AMS 52]
    51вҶ’theorem unitDistanceCounts_BddAbove (n : в„•) : BddAbove <| unitDistanceCounts n := by
    52вҶ’  unfold Erdos90.unitDistanceCounts
    53вҶ’  unfold Erdos90.unitDistancePairsCount
    54вҶ’  use n.choose 2
    55вҶ’  rintro _ вҹЁpoints, rfl, rflвҹ©
    56вҶ’  rw [points.card.choose_two_right]
    57вҶ’  gcongr
    58вҶ’  refine (card_filter_le _ _).trans_eq ?_
    59вҶ’  rw [offDiag_card, Nat.mul_sub_left_distrib, mul_one]
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’/--
    63вҶ’The **maximum number of unit distances** determined by any set of $n$ points in the plane.
    64вҶ’This function is often denoted as $u(n)$ in combinatorics.
    65вҶ’-/
    66вҶ’noncomputable def maxUnitDistances (n : в„•) : в„• :=
    67вҶ’  sSup (unitDistanceCounts n)
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’/--
    71вҶ’Does every set of $n$ distinct points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ contain at most
    72вҶ’$n^{1+O(\frac{1}{\log\log n})}$ many pairs which are distance $1$ apart?
    73вҶ’-/
    74вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 52]
    75вҶ’theorem erdos_90 : answer(sorry) вҶ” вҲғ (O : в„• вҶ’ в„қ) (hO : O =O[atTop] (fun n => 1 / (n : в„қ).log.log)),
    76вҶ’    (fun n => (maxUnitDistances n : в„қ)) =б¶ [atTop] fun (n : в„•) => (n : в„қ) ^ (1 + O n) := by
    77вҶ’  sorry
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’-- TODO(firsching): add the statements from the rest of the page.
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’end Erdos90
    82вҶ’

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 605
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/605](https://www.erdosproblems.com/605)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Is there some function $f(n)\to \infty$ as $n\to\infty$ such that there exist $n$ distinct
    25вҶ’points on the surface of a two-dimensional sphere with at least $f(n)n$ many pairs of
    26вҶ’points whose distances are the same?
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’This was solved by ErdЕ‘s, Hickerson, and Pach [EHP89], who showed $u_{\sqrt{2}}(n) \asymp
    29вҶ’n^{4/3}$ and $u_D(n) \gg n \log^* n$ for all $D > 1$. The lower bound was improved by
    30вҶ’Swanepoel and Valtr [SwVa04] to $u_D(n) \gg n\sqrt{\log n}$. The best upper bound for
    31вҶ’general $D$ is $u_D(n) \ll n^{4/3}$.
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’[EHP89] ErdЕ‘s, P., Hickerson, D., and Pach, J., _A problem of Leo Moser about repeated
    34вҶ’distances on the sphere_. Amer. Math. Monthly 96 (1989), 569вҖ“575.
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’[SwVa04] Swanepoel, K. J. and Valtr, P., _The unit distance problem on spheres_.
    37вҶ’In Towards a Theory of Geometric Graphs (2004), 145вҖ“160.
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’[Er85] ErdЕ‘s, P., _Problems and results in combinatorial geometry_. Discrete geometry and
    40вҶ’convexity (New York, 1982), Ann. New York Acad. Sci. 440 (1985), 1вҖ“11.
    41вҶ’-/
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’open Filter
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’namespace Erdos605
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’/-- The number of ordered pairs of distinct points from $A$ at Euclidean distance $d$. -/
    48вҶ’noncomputable def pairsAtDistance
    49вҶ’    (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))) (d : в„қ) : в„• :=
    50вҶ’  (A.offDiag.filter (fun p => dist p.1 p.2 = d)).card
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’/--
    53вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Problem 605 [Er85]:
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’There exists a function $f(n) \to \infty$ such that for all $n \geq 2$, one can place $n$ distinct
    56вҶ’points on the surface of a sphere in $\mathbb{R}^3$ with at least $f(n) \cdot n$ ordered pairs of
    57вҶ’points at some common distance. (Equivalently, at least $f(n) \cdot n / 2$ unordered pairs.)
    58вҶ’-/
    59вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]
    60вҶ’theorem erdos_605 : answer(True) вҶ”
    61вҶ’    вҲғ f : в„• вҶ’ в„қ, Tendsto f atTop atTop вҲ§
    62вҶ’      вҲҖ n : в„•, n вүҘ 2 вҶ’
    63вҶ’        вҲғ (R : в„қ) (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))),
    64вҶ’          R > 0 вҲ§ A.card = n вҲ§
    65вҶ’          (вҲҖ x вҲҲ A, вҖ–xвҖ– = R) вҲ§
    66вҶ’          вҲғ d : в„қ, (pairsAtDistance A d : в„қ) вүҘ f n * n := by
    67вҶ’  sorry
    68вҶ’
    69вҶ’end Erdos605
    70вҶ’

    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 217
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/217](https://www.erdosproblems.com/217)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’For which $n$ are there $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$, no three on a line and no four on a
    25вҶ’circle, which determine $n-1$ distinct distances and so that (in some ordering
    26вҶ’of the distances) the $i$-th distance occurs $i$ times?
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’An example with $n=4$ is an isosceles triangle with the point in the centre.
    29вҶ’ErdЕ‘s originally believed this was impossible for $n \geq 5$, but Pomerance
    30вҶ’constructed a set with $n = 5$, and PalГЎsti proved such sets exist for all $n \leq 8$.
    31вҶ’ErdЕ‘s believed this is impossible for all sufficiently large $n$.
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’[Er83c] [Er87b] [Er97e]
    34вҶ’-/
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’namespace Erdos217
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’/-- Three points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are collinear if the cross product of $(q - p)$ and $(r - p)$
    39вҶ’vanishes, i.e. the signed area of the triangle is zero. -/
    40вҶ’def Collinear3 (p q r : EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)) : Prop :=
    41вҶ’  (q 0 - p 0) * (r 1 - p 1) = (q 1 - p 1) * (r 0 - p 0)
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’/-- A finite point set in $\mathbb{R}^2$ has no three collinear points. -/
    44вҶ’def NoThreeCollinear (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    45вҶ’  вҲҖ p вҲҲ S, вҲҖ q вҲҲ S, вҲҖ r вҲҲ S, p вү  q вҶ’ p вү  r вҶ’ q вү  r вҶ’ В¬Collinear3 p q r
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’/-- Four points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ are concyclic if they are all equidistant from some center. -/
    48вҶ’def Concyclic4 (pвӮҒ pвӮӮ pвӮғ pвӮ„ : EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)) : Prop :=
    49вҶ’  вҲғ (c : EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)) (r : в„қ),
    50вҶ’    dist pвӮҒ c = r вҲ§ dist pвӮӮ c = r вҲ§ dist pвӮғ c = r вҲ§ dist pвӮ„ c = r
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’/-- A finite point set in $\mathbb{R}^2$ has no four concyclic points. -/
    53вҶ’def NoFourConcyclic (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    54вҶ’  вҲҖ pвӮҒ вҲҲ S, вҲҖ pвӮӮ вҲҲ S, вҲҖ pвӮғ вҲҲ S, вҲҖ pвӮ„ вҲҲ S,
    55вҶ’    pвӮҒ вү  pвӮӮ вҶ’ pвӮҒ вү  pвӮғ вҶ’ pвӮҒ вү  pвӮ„ вҶ’ pвӮӮ вү  pвӮғ вҶ’ pвӮӮ вү  pвӮ„ вҶ’ pвӮғ вү  pвӮ„ вҶ’
    56вҶ’    В¬Concyclic4 pвӮҒ pвӮӮ pвӮғ pвӮ„
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’/-- A set $S$ of $n$ points has the distance multiplicity property if there exist $n-1$
    59вҶ’distinct pairwise distances such that (in some ordering) the $j$-th distance
    60вҶ’occurs exactly $j$ times as an unordered pair. We count ordered pairs from
    61вҶ’`S.offDiag`, so the $j$-th distance yields $2(j+1)$ ordered pairs (since
    62вҶ’`Fin (n-1)` is 0-indexed). -/
    63вҶ’def HasDistanceMultiplicityProperty (S : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2))) : Prop :=
    64вҶ’  вҲғ (d : Fin (S.card - 1) вҶ’ в„қ),
    65вҶ’    Function.Injective d вҲ§
    66вҶ’    (вҲҖ p вҲҲ S, вҲҖ q вҲҲ S, p вү  q вҶ’ вҲғ i, dist p q = d i) вҲ§
    67вҶ’    (вҲҖ i : Fin (S.card - 1),
    68вҶ’      (S.offDiag.filter (fun pq => dist pq.1 pq.2 = d i)).card = 2 * (i.val + 1))
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’/--
    71вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Conjecture (Problem 217) [Er83c, Er87b, Er97e]:
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’For all sufficiently large $n$, there does not exist a set of $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$,
    74вҶ’no three on a line and no four on a circle, which determine $n-1$ distinct
    75вҶ’distances such that (in some ordering) the $i$-th distance occurs exactly $i$ times.
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’Known: such configurations exist for all $n \leq 8$ (PalГЎsti). ErdЕ‘s conjectured
    78вҶ’this is impossible for all sufficiently large $n$.
    79вҶ’-/
    80вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 5 52]

    17вҶ’import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# ErdЕ‘s Problem 991
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/991](https://www.erdosproblems.com/991)
    23вҶ’
    24вҶ’Suppose $A = \{w_1, \ldots, w_n\} \subset S^2$ maximises $\prod_{i<j} |w_i - w_j|$ over all
    25вҶ’possible sets of size $n$ (such sets are called Fekete points).
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’Is it true that
    28вҶ’$$
    29вҶ’  \max_C \left| |A \cap C| - \alpha_C \cdot n \right| = o(n),
    30вҶ’$$
    31вҶ’where the maximum is taken over all spherical caps $C$ and $\alpha_C$ is the area
    32вҶ’of $C$ (normalised so that the entire sphere has area $1$)?
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’This was proved. Brauchart [Br08] showed $O(n^{3/4})$ and MarzoвҖ“Mas [MaMa21]
    35вҶ’improved this to $O(n^{2/3})$.
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’[Br08] Brauchart, J. S., *Optimal discrete Riesz energy and discrepancy*,
    38вҶ’Uniform Distribution Theory 3 (2008), no. 1, 47вҖ“78.
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’[MaMa21] Marzo, J. and Mas, A., *Discrepancy of minimal Riesz energy points*,
    41вҶ’J. Approx. Theory 264 (2021), 105538.
    42вҶ’-/
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’open Classical Finset BigOperators
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’namespace Erdos991
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’/-- The product of pairwise distances of a finite set of points. -/
    49вҶ’noncomputable def pairwiseDistProd (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))) : в„қ :=
    50вҶ’  вҲҸ p вҲҲ P.offDiag, вҖ–p.1 - p.2вҖ–
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’/-- A finite set of points on $S^2$ maximises the pairwise distance product
    53вҶ’among all sets of the same cardinality on $S^2$. -/
    54вҶ’def IsMaxPairwiseDist (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))) : Prop :=
    55вҶ’  (вҲҖ p вҲҲ P, вҖ–pвҖ– = 1) вҲ§
    56вҶ’  вҲҖ (Q : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))),
    57вҶ’    (вҲҖ q вҲҲ Q, вҖ–qвҖ– = 1) вҶ’ Q.card = P.card вҶ’
    58вҶ’    pairwiseDistProd Q вүӨ pairwiseDistProd P
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’/--
    61вҶ’ErdЕ‘s Problem 991 [Er64b]:
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’If $A = \{w_1, \ldots, w_n\} \subset S^2$ are Fekete points (maximising the product of
    64вҶ’pairwise distances), then the spherical cap discrepancy of $A$ is $o(n)$:
    65вҶ’for every $\varepsilon > 0$, if $n$ is large enough and $P$ is any set of $n$ Fekete points,
    66вҶ’then for every spherical cap $C(v,t) = \{x \in S^2 : \langle x,v \rangle \geq t\}$,
    67вҶ’$$
    68вҶ’  \left| |P \cap C(v,t)| - \frac{1-t}{2} \cdot n \right| \leq \varepsilon \cdot n.
    69вҶ’$$
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’Here $\frac{1-t}{2}$ is the normalised surface area of the cap $C(v,t)$ on $S^2$.
    72вҶ’-/
    73вҶ’@[category research solved, AMS 31 52]
    74вҶ’theorem erdos_991 :
    75вҶ’    answer(True) вҶ”
    76вҶ’    вҲҖ Оө : в„қ, Оө > 0 вҶ’
    77вҶ’    вҲғ NвӮҖ : в„•, вҲҖ (P : Finset (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3))),
    78вҶ’      IsMaxPairwiseDist P вҶ’
    79вҶ’      P.card вүҘ NвӮҖ вҶ’
    80вҶ’      вҲҖ (v : EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 3)) (t : в„қ),
    81вҶ’        вҖ–vвҖ– = 1 вҶ’ -1 вүӨ t вҶ’ t вүӨ 1 вҶ’
    82вҶ’        |((P.filter (fun x => @inner в„қ _ _ x v вүҘ t)).card : в„қ) -
    83вҶ’          (1 - t) / 2 * (P.card : в„қ)| вүӨ Оө * (P.card : в„қ) := by
    84вҶ’  sorry
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’end Erdos991
    87вҶ’

    20вҶ’# Determinantal conjecture
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Determinantal_conjecture)
    23вҶ’-/
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’/- Formalisation note: Here, we represent the two normal matrices as $U_1 D_1 U_1^*$ and
    26вҶ’$U_2 D_2 U_2^*$ respectively, where $U_1, U_2$ are unitary and $D_1, D_2$
    27вҶ’are diagonal. This is an universal form of a normal matrix,
    28вҶ’allowing to retrieve $\lambda (A)_i$ as ${D_1}_{i,i}$, whereas the current mathlib
    29вҶ’doesn't support obtaining the vector of eigenvalues from a general normal
    30вҶ’non-Hermitian matrix. -/
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’namespace DeterminantalConjecture
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’/--
    35вҶ’Does the determinant of the sum $A + B$ of two $n \times n$ normal
    36вҶ’complex matrices $A$ and $B$ always lie in the convex hull
    37вҶ’of the $n!$ points $\prod_i (\lambda(A)_i + \lambda(B)_{\sigma(i)})$?
    38вҶ’Here the numbers $\lambda(A)_i$ and $\lambda(B)_i$ are
    39вҶ’the eigenvalues of $A$ and $B$, and $\sigma$ is an element of the symmetric
    40вҶ’group $S_n$.
    41вҶ’-/
    42вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 15]
    43вҶ’theorem determinantal_conjecture
    44вҶ’    (n : Type) [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n]
    45вҶ’    (d1 d2 : n вҶ’ в„Ӯ) (U1 U2 : unitary (Matrix n n в„Ӯ)) :
    46вҶ’    (U1 * Matrix.diagonal d1 * star U1 + U2 * Matrix.diagonal d2 * star U2).det
    47вҶ’      вҲҲ convexHull в„қ { вҲҸ i, (d1 i + d2 (Пғ i)) | Пғ : Equiv.Perm n } := by
    48вҶ’  sorry
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’end DeterminantalConjecture

    19вҶ’/-!
    20вҶ’# Ben Green's Open Problem 57
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’*Reference:* [Ben Green's Open Problem 57](https://people.maths.ox.ac.uk/greenbj/papers/open-problems.pdf#section.8 Problem 57)
    23вҶ’-/
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’namespace Green57
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’open scoped Pointwise
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’noncomputable section
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’variable (G : Type*) [AddCommGroup G] [Fintype G] [DecidableEq G]
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’/-- Uniform average over pairs `(xвӮҒ, xвӮӮ)` in `G Г— G`, with the third variable determined by
    34вҶ’the relation `xвӮҒ + xвӮӮ + xвӮғ = g`. -/
    35вҶ’def tripleKernel (fвӮҒ fвӮӮ fвӮғ : G Г— G вҶ’ в„қ) : G вҶ’ в„қ := fun g =>
    36вҶ’  let cardG : в„қ := Fintype.card G
    37вҶ’  (cardG ^ 2)вҒ»В№ *
    38вҶ’      вҲ‘ xвӮҒ : G, вҲ‘ xвӮӮ : G,
    39вҶ’        fвӮҒ (xвӮӮ, g - xвӮҒ - xвӮӮ) * fвӮӮ (xвӮҒ, g - xвӮҒ - xвӮӮ) * fвӮғ (xвӮҒ, xвӮӮ)
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’/-- The generating family of functions for `ОҰ(G)`. -/
    42вҶ’def baseОҰ : Set (G вҶ’ в„қ) :=
    43вҶ’  {ПҶ | вҲғ fвӮҒ fвӮӮ fвӮғ : G Г— G вҶ’ в„қ,
    44вҶ’    (вҲҖ p, |fвӮҒ p| вүӨ 1) вҲ§ (вҲҖ p, |fвӮӮ p| вүӨ 1) вҲ§ (вҲҖ p, |fвӮғ p| вүӨ 1) вҲ§
    45вҶ’    ПҶ = tripleKernel G fвӮҒ fвӮӮ fвӮғ}
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’/-- The generating family of functions for `ОҰвҖІ(G)`, where the third kernel depends only on
    48вҶ’`xвӮҒ + xвӮӮ`. -/
    49вҶ’def baseОҰ' : Set (G вҶ’ в„қ) :=
    50вҶ’  {ПҶ | вҲғ fвӮҒ fвӮӮ : G Г— G вҶ’ в„қ, вҲғ h : G вҶ’ в„қ,
    51вҶ’    (вҲҖ p, |fвӮҒ p| вүӨ 1) вҲ§ (вҲҖ p, |fвӮӮ p| вүӨ 1) вҲ§ (вҲҖ x, |h x| вүӨ 1) вҲ§
    52вҶ’    ПҶ = tripleKernel G fвӮҒ fвӮӮ (fun p => h (p.1 + p.2))}
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’/-- The space `ОҰ(G)` of convex combinations of kernels from `baseОҰ`. -/
    55вҶ’def ОҰ : Set (G вҶ’ в„қ) :=
    56вҶ’  convexHull в„қ (baseОҰ G)
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’/-- The restricted space `ОҰвҖІ(G)` of convex combinations of kernels from `baseОҰ'`. -/
    59вҶ’def ОҰ' : Set (G вҶ’ в„қ) :=
    60вҶ’  convexHull в„қ (baseОҰ' G)
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’/--
    63вҶ’Is it true that for every finite abelian group $G$ the convex hulls $\Phi(G)$ and $\Phi'(G)$,
    64вҶ’obtained from kernels $\phi(g) = \mathbb{E}_{x_1 + x_2 + x_3 = g} f_1(x_2, x_3)
    65вҶ’      f_2(x_1, x_3) f_3(x_1, x_2)$ with $\lVert f_i \rVert_\infty \le 1$, still coincide when the
    66вҶ’third kernel is required to depend only on $x_1 + x_2$?
    67вҶ’-/
    68вҶ’@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    69вҶ’theorem green_57 :
    70вҶ’  answer(sorry) вҶ”
    71вҶ’    вҲҖ (G : Type) [AddCommGroup G] [Fintype G] [DecidableEq G],
    72вҶ’      ОҰ G = ОҰ' G := by
    73вҶ’  sorry
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’end
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’end Green57
    78вҶ’
